executive_summary: The target web application has multiple security vulnerabilities, including SQL injection and missing security headers. Immediate action is required to mitigate these risks.

sqlmap: SQLMap detected several SQL injection vulnerabilities in the 'id' parameter.
Potential exploitation methods were attempted but encountered limitations.
zap: Multiple medium and low-risk issues identified, including missing security headers and version information exposure.
No critical or high-risk vulnerabilities found based on ZAP scans.
nikto: Directory indexing, configuration file leakage, and outdated Apache version are notable findings.
PHP session cookies do not have the 'HttpOnly' flag set, potentially exposing user data to XSS attacks.
virustotal: VirusTotal scan was skipped due to local/private URL issues.

issues.title: SQL Injection - Blind Boolean-Based
impact: Potential unauthorized data manipulation or complete database compromise.
remediation: Implement parameter validation and input sanitization. Consider using prepared statements in the backend code.

issues.title: SQL Injection - Error-Based
impact: Potential unauthorized data manipulation or complete database compromise.
remediation: Implement parameter validation and input sanitization. Consider using prepared statements in the backend code.

issues.title: SQL Injection - Time-Based Blind
impact: Potential unauthorized data manipulation or complete database compromise.
remediation: Implement parameter validation and input sanitization. Consider using prepared statements in the backend code.

issues.title: SQL Injection - UNION-Based
impact: Potential unauthorized data manipulation or complete database compromise.
remediation: Implement parameter validation and input sanitization. Consider using prepared statements in the backend code.

issues.title: Missing Anti-clickjacking Header
remediation: Set the X-Frame-Options header to 'DENY' or 'SAMEORIGIN'.

issues.title: Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set
remediation: Implement a CSP header to mitigate potential risks of cross-site scripting (XSS) attacks.

issues.title: Server Leaks Version Information via 'Server' HTTP Response Header Field
remediation: Remove or obfuscate the 'Server' header in HTTP responses.

issues.title: Missing HttpOnly Flag on Cookies
evidence: Cookie PHPSESSID created without the 'HttpOnly' flag.
Cookie security created without the 'HttpOnly' flag.
impact: Potential for cross-site scripting (XSS) attacks that could steal session cookies.
remediation: Ensure all sensitive cookies have the 'HttpOnly' flag set to prevent client-side access.

issues.title: Directory Listing Enabled
impact: Potential for directory traversal and access to sensitive information.
remediation: Disable directory listing or restrict access through .htaccess or similar mechanisms.

issues.title: Outdated Apache Version
impact: Potential for known vulnerabilities to exist in the version.
remediation: Upgrade Apache to a more recent and secure version.

issues.title: Admin Login Page Found
impact: Potential unauthorized access to the administrative section of the application.
remediation: Implement strong authentication mechanisms and secure password storage practices for admin accounts.

recommended_next_steps: Address all critical injection vulnerabilities by implementing robust input validation and sanitization.
Configure security headers such as X-Frame-Options, Content-Security-Policy, and remove or obfuscate the 'Server' header.
Restrict directory listings to prevent unauthorized access to sensitive files.
Upgrade Apache to a more recent version for improved security.
limitations: VirusTotal scan was skipped due to local/private URL issues.
